title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on the 10-day injured list with a sports hernia by the Red Sox.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story on the roster.
Red Sox unsure if Story will require surgery, potential recovery time is 6-10 weeks.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man died after his pickup truck collided head-on with a school bus in Marlborough, New Hampshire.
The school bus, carrying 13 students, had a driver and several students sent to hospitals; no serious injuries reported.
The crash on Route 12 remains under investigation by New Hampshire State Police, Marlborough police, and fire officials.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th inning led the Braves to a 3-2 win over the Red Sox.
Starting pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs, but showed solid performance with six strikeouts in five innings.
Boston's offensive struggles continued, leaving seven men on base and going 1-for-3 with runners in scoring position.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots reunion with Stefon Diggs is unlikely due to the potential trade for A.J. Brown.
Diggs, 32, expressed interest in returning to New England but would conflict with team's salary cap plans.
Patriots' pending A.J. Brown trade would burden them with a $32 million AAV contract.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday afternoon near 965 Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester.
Boston police describe the missing teen as Black, 5'8", thin build, with brown eyes and black hair.
Authorities ask anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712 or submit tips anonymously.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, was arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The victims, both 42, suffered serious injuries, with the mother experiencing life-threatening wounds from the shooting.
Broomes faces charges including assault with intent to murder, and is held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox pitchers Sale, Rodriguez, Harrison, Wacha, and Eovaldi have combined for a 2.74 ERA in 2026.
Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber continue to produce strong performances for their respective teams.
The envisioned lineup of ex-Red Sox includes Betts, Bregman, Schwarber, and Bogaerts, with standout pitcher Chris Sale.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony, Red Sox outfielder, removed his wrist brace and played catch before a game against the Braves on Friday.
Anthony, on the injured list since May 7 with a wrist sprain, has yet to resume swinging a bat but is progressing well.
His performance improved in May before the injury, batting .385 with a .467 on-base percentage in four games.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A white Jeep accidentally drove into BrightPath daycare in Norwood, forcing its temporary closure.
No injuries were reported among the children, staff, families, or driver after the incident.
The building suffered moderate damage; the daycare will remain closed pending structural evaluation.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict a 10-7 record for the New England Patriots for the 2026 season.
The Patriots face a challenging schedule with opponents like Seattle, Denver, and Chicago.
Some experts cite the Patriots' offseason improvements but expect fewer wins than last season's 14-3 record.
